subjectAreas.name: Jesus, the Gospels, and Acts
subjectAreas.description: Covers the canonical Gospels, Acts, major New Testament figures, and traditions about Jesus' life, teachings, death, burial, and resurrection.

subjectAreas.name: Paul and the Epistles
subjectAreas.description: Covers Paul's life, mission, communities, letters, theology, ethics, and the broader collection of New Testament epistles.

subjectAreas.name: Hebrew Bible and Early Judaism
subjectAreas.description: Covers Hebrew Bible texts, traditions, historical questions, and the early Jewish context for Jesus and early Christianity.

subjectAreas.name: Early Christianity and Church History
subjectAreas.description: Covers Christian origins, early Christian diversity, church fathers, persecution, martyrdom, and church history after the New Testament period.

subjectAreas.name: Non-Canonical Texts and Heresy
subjectAreas.description: Covers non-canonical Christian writings, Gnostic texts, heresy, and early Christian traditions outside the New Testament.

subjectAreas.name: Manuscripts, Canon, and Bible Translation
subjectAreas.description: Covers how biblical texts were copied, collected, canonized, translated, and reconstructed from manuscripts and other textual witnesses.

subjectAreas.name: Authorship, Forgery, and Historical Methods
subjectAreas.description: Covers who wrote biblical and early Christian texts, how traditions were transmitted, how scholars evaluate sources, historical methods, and forgery.

subjectAreas.name: Theology, Belief, and Supernatural Claims
subjectAreas.description: Covers theological beliefs and religious questions, including Christology, salvation, atonement, judgment, faith, doubt, miracles, visions, and claims about divine or supernatural activity.

subjectAreas.name: Afterlife, Revelation, End Times, and Suffering
subjectAreas.description: Covers ancient and Christian views of life after death, Revelation, end-time expectations, resurrection, heaven and hell, divine judgment, suffering, and evil.

subjectAreas.name: Ancient World, Ethics, and Social Issues
subjectAreas.description: Covers the ancient social, cultural, and political world around biblical texts, including Roman context, ethics, wealth and poverty, gender, and sexuality.

subjectAreas.name: Books, Teaching, Media, and Blog Features
subjectAreas.description: Covers Bart's books, teaching, courses, media appearances, scholarship, public engagement, blog features, personal posts, and the In a Nutshell series.
categories: Academic Life, Scholars, and Public Debate
